title: Functions missing [CmdletBinding()] lose advanced parameter support
description: Functions without the [CmdletBinding()] attribute are 'simple functions' that lack support for -Verbose, -Debug, -WhatIf, -Confirm, -ErrorAction, -ErrorVariable, and other common parameters. This limits caller control over error handling, logging verbosity, and dry-run testing. All exported module functions should include [CmdletBinding()] (or [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess=$true)] for state-changing operations). Private helper functions and script-level code may omit it.
detection_method: AST scan for FunctionDefinitionAst nodes that lack CmdletBinding attribute in exported module functions
preventionRule: All exported module functions should include [CmdletBinding()] in their param() block. State-changing functions should use [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess=$true)]. Private/internal helpers are exempt.
remedy: Add [CmdletBinding()] before the param() block. For functions that modify state, use [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess=$true)] and add if ($PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess(...)) guards around destructive operations.
